The coach gave sound advice before the match.
He swam across the river without stopping.
She searched the web for information about the course.

sentences.en: The young athlete worked hard to advance to the national level.

sentences.en: Her average mark in the course was well above the class average.

sentences.en: Good behavior in public reflects a person's true personality.

sentences.en: He fell behind the other runners but refused to give up.
sentences.bn: সে অন্য দৌড়বিদদের পেছনে পড়ে গেল কিন্তু হাল ছেড়ে দিতে অস্বীকার করল।

sentences.en: She enrolled in an online course to improve her web design skills.

sentences.en: The coach advised them to lower their speed on the wet track.

sentences.en: The final match between the two teams attracted a huge crowd.

sentences.en: A warm and friendly personality can make a real difference in any team.

sentences.en: The forward advanced past two defenders and shot the ball into the net.

sentences.en: The sound of the crowd cheering gave the athlete extra mental strength.

sentences.en: She can swim an average of two kilometers every morning without stopping.
sentences.bn: সে প্রতিদিন সকালে গড়ে দুই কিলোমিটার না থেমে সাঁতার কাটতে পারে।

sentences.en: He searched the web for sound advice on how to advance in his course.
sentences.bn: সে তার কোর্সে কীভাবে এগিয়ে যেতে হবে তার সঠিক পরামর্শের জন্য ওয়েবে খুঁজল।

opts: to move forward or make progress
quiz.explanation: Advance means to move forward, make progress, or develop to a higher level.

opts: a person trained in sports or physical activities
quiz.explanation: Athlete means a person who is trained or skilled in sports and physical exercise.

opts: the typical or ordinary level or a middle value
quiz.explanation: Average means the typical or normal standard, or a value calculated as the middle of a set.

opts: the way a person or animal acts
quiz.explanation: Behavior means the way in which a person, animal, or thing acts or conducts itself.

opts: at the back of or further back than
quiz.explanation: Behind means at the back of something or in a position further back than another.

opts: a series of lessons or a path or route
quiz.explanation: Course means a series of lessons or lectures on a subject, or a path taken to reach a destination.

opts: to move something down or reduce
quiz.explanation: Lower means to move something to a less high position or to reduce an amount or level.

opts: a sports game or something that fits another
quiz.explanation: Match means a sports competition between two people or teams, or two things that are equal or similar.

opts: a person who belongs to a group or organization
quiz.explanation: Member means a person, animal, or thing that belongs to a particular group or organization.

opts: relating to the mind or thinking
quiz.explanation: Mental means relating to the mind, thinking, or intellectual processes.

opts: a person who drives a vehicle
a person who travels in a vehicle but does not drive
a team member in a match
quiz.explanation: Passenger means a person who is traveling in a vehicle, aircraft, or ship but is not the driver.

opts: the combination of qualities that make a person unique
quiz.explanation: Personality means the combination of characteristics that form a person's distinctive character.

opts: a piece of writing expressing feelings using rhythm
quiz.explanation: Poem means a piece of writing in which words are chosen for their sound and meaning, often with rhythm.

opts: a long thin piece of wood or metal or either end of the earth
quiz.explanation: Pole means a long thin rounded piece of wood or metal, or either of the two ends of the Earth's axis.

opts: to take something away from its place
quiz.explanation: Remove means to take something away from the place where it is.

opts: the condition of being in danger
the condition of being protected from danger
quiz.explanation: Safety means the condition of being protected from or unlikely to cause danger or injury.

opts: to fire a weapon or kick toward a goal
quiz.explanation: Shoot means to fire a bullet or arrow, or to kick or throw a ball toward a goal.

opts: a vibration that is heard or the word meaning healthy
quiz.explanation: Sound means vibrations that travel through air and are heard by the ear, or something that is reliable and healthy.

opts: to move through water using the body
quiz.explanation: Swim means to propel the body through water by moving the limbs.

opts: a network of threads or the internet
quiz.explanation: Web means a network of fine threads made by a spider, or the World Wide Web which is the internet.

story.title: The Athlete Behind the Web
story.body: Rafi was not your average athlete. While most of his teammates advanced through training by focusing only on physical strength, Rafi believed that mental preparation was just as important. He was a member of his school's swimming team and had been practicing every morning since the age of nine — swimming across the full length of the pool before most passengers on the early bus had even woken up. His behavior on and off the course was always respectful and disciplined, and his personality made him a natural leader. Coaches regarded him highly and often gave him sound advice about how to lower his average lap time without risking his safety. One evening, Rafi sat beside a pole at the edge of the pool and searched the web for information about advanced swimming techniques. A poem he had written about his sport was open in another tab — he wrote poems sometimes to reflect on his mental state and process his emotions. As he read through articles, he came across a coaching course that promised to help swimmers advance to a higher level. The price was beyond what his family could afford, but rather than fall behind his goal, he wrote a message to the course director explaining his situation. He removed all hesitation and communicated honestly about who he was, what he had achieved, and why he believed the training was worth pursuing. Three days later, he received a response. The director had read his message and was moved by his personality and determination. He offered Rafi a full scholarship. On the day of the national match, Rafi stood at the starting pole, breathing steadily. The sound of the crowd filled the hall. When the signal came, he shot forward into the water like an arrow. He did not look behind. He advanced stroke by stroke, breath by breath, his mental focus unwavering. When he touched the wall at the finish, the crowd erupted. He had not only won — he had set a new average record for his age group. His coach removed his stopwatch from around his neck and placed it around Rafi's as a symbol of trust. Later, a journalist asked him what had made the difference. Rafi smiled and said, 'Mental strength, sound preparation, and never being afraid to ask for help — even if you have to search the web to find it.'

comprehension.q: What made Rafi different from average athletes?
opts: he believed mental preparation was just as important as physical strength
he never fell behind in any match

comprehension.q: What did Rafi find when he searched the web?
opts: a coaching course to help him advance to a higher level

comprehension.q: How did Rafi respond to the course being too expensive?
opts: he gave up on his goal
he removed all hesitation and wrote honestly to the course director
he borrowed money from a member
he searched for a lower course

comprehension.q: What happened on the day of the national match?
opts: Rafi won and set a new average record for his age group
the match was removed from the schedule

comprehension.q: What did Rafi say made the difference in his performance?
opts: mental strength, sound preparation, and asking for help
a new swimming pole and course

listening.title: A Coach and an Athlete Before the Big Match
listening.script:
A: How are you feeling before the match today?
B: A bit nervous. I keep thinking about the last time I fell behind in the final lap.
A: That is normal. Every athlete feels that before a big match.
B: I searched the web last night for tips on how to advance faster.
A: Sound advice is better found from experience than from a web search.
B: You are right. What do you think I should focus on?
A: Your mental state. Remove all negative thoughts from your mind before you step on the course.
B: How do I do that?
A: Breathe slowly. Focus on your behavior in the water — each stroke, each movement.
B: What about the other members of the team? Some of them are well above average.
A: Do not compare. Every athlete has their own course to follow.
B: The safety rules for the pool — should I review them?
A: You already know them. Just make sure you do not shoot forward too fast at the start and risk your form.
B: What about the passenger seats — will there be a big crowd watching?
A: Yes, the hall will be full. Use their sound to fuel your mental strength.
B: I wrote a poem last night to calm myself. Is that strange for an athlete?
A: Not at all. Personality and creativity are part of what makes a great athlete.
B: The pole position I draw today will determine my lane. I hope it is a good one.
A: Whatever lane you get, advance from it with everything you have.
B: I will lower my average time today. I am sure of it.
A: I believe you. Now go warm up and remove all doubt from your mind.
B: Thank you, coach. Your sound advice always helps.

comprehension.q: What was the athlete nervous about?
opts: falling behind in the final lap like last time

comprehension.q: What does the coach tell the athlete to remove before the match?
opts: all negative thoughts from the mind

comprehension.q: How does the coach say the athlete should use the crowd?
opts: use the sound of the crowd to fuel mental strength
ask members of the crowd for advice
remove themselves from the crowd's view

comprehension.q: What does the coach say about the athlete writing a poem?
opts: it is a waste of time for an athlete
personality and creativity are part of what makes a great athlete
web searches are better than poems

speaking.topic: Describing a Sport or Physical Activity You Enjoy
speaking.prompt: Talk about a sport or physical activity you enjoy. Use words like athlete, advance, match, swim, shoot, mental, safety, average, course, and behavior.
speaking.example: I enjoy swimming more than any other sport. I am not an average swimmer — I have been practicing since I was a child and I have advanced through several courses over the years. Swimming is not only a physical activity but also a mental one. When I am in the water, I remove all stress from my mind and focus only on each stroke. Safety is always important — I never swim without a lifeguard present. Before a match, I think carefully about my behavior in the water and how I can lower my average lap time. I regard every swim session as a step forward. One day I hope to compete at a higher level and shoot for a national record.
tips: Name the sport or activity and say how long you have been doing it
Use advance and course to talk about your progress and training
Mention the mental side of the sport and how it affects your behavior
Talk about safety rules or habits you follow
Use average and match to describe your level and competitions
End with a goal you want to achieve in the sport
